name: Patch Antenna Gain Optimization
description: Tests basic optimize() call with maximizeGain objective on a patch antenna
prompt: Optimize a microstrip patch antenna at 2.4 GHz to maximize its gain. Use Length and Width as design variables with ±20% bounds around the designed values. Run 3 iterations. For post-optimization validation, evaluate only the gain at the design frequency. Do not compute s-parameters. Provide the complete MATLAB script.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses optimize() function
assertions.expected: optimize

assertions.description: Bounds are two-row cell array {lb1, lb2; ub1, ub2}
assertions.expected: bounds formatted as two-row cell array where columns correspond to Length and Width; NOT a numeric matrix [lb;ub] or single-column cell

assertions.description: design() called with patchMicrostrip at 2.4 GHz and its output passed directly to optimize() at the same frequency
assertions.expected: design(patchMicrostrip, 2.4e9) or equivalent called, and the resulting antenna object used as first argument to optimize() with 2.4e9 as frequency; NOT a manually constructed antenna or a different frequency in optimize()

name: Yagi-Uda Gain Optimization
description: Tests optimize() with maximizeGain on a multi-element antenna using correct property names
prompt: Optimize a Yagi-Uda antenna at 300 MHz for maximum gain. Use DirectorLength and ReflectorLength as design variables. Run 3 iterations. For post-optimization validation, evaluate only the gain at the design frequency. Do not compute s-parameters. Provide the complete MATLAB script.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses maximizeGain objective
assertions.expected: maximizeGain

assertions.description: Uses correct yagiUda properties (DirectorLength, ReflectorLength, not Length)
assertions.expected: DirectorLength and/or ReflectorLength in design variables

assertions.description: design() called with yagiUda at 300 MHz and its output passed directly to optimize() at the same frequency
assertions.expected: design(yagiUda, 300e6) or equivalent called, and the resulting antenna object used as first argument to optimize() with 300e6 as frequency; NOT a manually constructed antenna or a different frequency in optimize()

assertions.description: Bounds formatted as two-row cell array matching design variable order
assertions.expected: Bounds passed as {lb1, lb2; ub1, ub2} cell array where columns correspond to DirectorLength and ReflectorLength; NOT a numeric matrix [lb;ub] or single-column cell

name: Dipole Gain Optimization with Area Analysis Constraint
description: Tests optimize() with maximizeGain objective and an Area analysis constraint passed as string in Constraints cell array
prompt: Optimize a dipole antenna at 750 MHz to maximize gain. Use Length and Width as design variables with ±30% bounds. Add an analysis constraint that the antenna area must be less than 0.01 square meters. Run 3 iterations. For post-optimization validation, evaluate only the gain at the design frequency. Do not compute s-parameters. Provide the complete MATLAB script.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses optimize() function
assertions.expected: optimize

assertions.description: Area constraint passed as string in cell array using Constraints name-value argument
assertions.expected: Constraints={'Area < 0.01'} or equivalent cell array of constraint strings with an Area constraint passed to optimize() via the Constraints name-value argument; NOT initGeomConstraint, NOT a manual check in a loop or post-hoc validation

assertions.description: design() called with dipole at 750 MHz and its output passed directly to optimize() at the same frequency
assertions.expected: design(dipole, 750e6) or equivalent called, and the resulting antenna object used as first argument to optimize() with 750e6 as frequency; NOT a manually constructed antenna or a different frequency in optimize()

assertions.description: Bounds formatted as two-row cell array matching design variable order
assertions.expected: Bounds passed as {lb1, lb2; ub1, ub2} cell array where columns correspond to Length and Width; NOT a numeric matrix [lb;ub] or single-column cell

name: Patch Optimization with Geometric Constraint
description: Tests optimize() with a geometric constraint using initGeomConstraint and TR-SADEA algorithm to enforce a relationship between design variables
prompt: Optimize a microstrip patch antenna at 70 MHz to maximize gain using the TR-SADEA algorithm. Use Length and Width as design variables with ±30% bounds. Add a geometric constraint that Length must be less than or equal to 2 times the Width. Run 3 iterations. For post-optimization validation, evaluate only the gain at the design frequency. Do not compute s-parameters. Provide the complete MATLAB script.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses initGeomConstraint
assertions.expected: initGeomConstraint

assertions.description: Geometric constraint struct with A matrix and b vector encodes Length <= 2*Width correctly
assertions.expected: initGeomConstraint called to create a struct; gc.A set to a row encoding Length - 2*Width <= 0 (coefficients [1, -2] matching design variable order {Length, Width}); gc.b = 0; passed to optimize() via GeometricConstraints name-value argument; NOT a string in Constraints={...}, NOT a manual check

assertions.description: design() called with patchMicrostrip at 70 MHz and its output passed directly to optimize() at the same frequency
assertions.expected: design(patchMicrostrip, 70e6) or equivalent called, and the resulting antenna object used as first argument to optimize() with 70e6 (or 7e7) as frequency; NOT a manually constructed antenna or a different frequency in optimize()

assertions.description: Bounds formatted as two-row cell array matching design variable order
assertions.expected: Bounds passed as {lb1, lb2; ub1, ub2} cell array where columns correspond to Length and Width; NOT a numeric matrix [lb;ub] or single-column cell

assertions.description: Uses TR-SADEA algorithm via UseAlgorithm name-value argument
assertions.expected: optimize() called with UseAlgorithm="TR-SADEA" name-value argument; NOT default SADEA, NOT a manual optimizer object

name: Reflector Front-to-Back Optimization
description: Tests optimize() with frontToBackRatio objective on a reflector antenna
prompt: Optimize a reflector antenna at 70 MHz for best front-to-back ratio. Use Spacing and GroundPlaneLength as design variables. Run 3 iterations and report the fitness. For post-optimization validation, evaluate only the radiation pattern at the design frequency. Do not compute s-parameters. Provide the complete MATLAB script.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses front-to-back objective string
assertions.expected: frontToBackRatio

assertions.description: Reports fitness using getBestMemberData from the optimizer info object
assertions.expected: getBestMemberData() or equivalent called on optinfo/optimizer object to extract best fitness value; NOT manual re-evaluation or prose-only reporting

assertions.description: design() called with reflector at 70 MHz and its output passed directly to optimize() at the same frequency
assertions.expected: design(reflector, 70e6) or equivalent called, and the resulting antenna object used as first argument to optimize() with 70e6 (or 7e7) as frequency; NOT a manually constructed antenna or a different frequency in optimize()

assertions.description: Bounds formatted as two-row cell array matching design variable order
assertions.expected: Bounds passed as {lb1, lb2; ub1, ub2} cell array where columns correspond to Spacing and GroundPlaneLength; NOT a numeric matrix [lb;ub] or single-column cell
